description_wiki: Hiroshima Prefecture (広島県, Hiroshima-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [çi.ɾo.ɕi.ma, -maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

surface_forms: a strolling flute player; usually a Buddhist monk
description_wiki: The Komusō (虚無僧) ("priest of nothingness" or "monk of emptiness") were wandering non-monastic lay Buddhists from the warrior-class (samurai and rōnin) who were noted for wearing straw basket hats called tengai and playing the shakuhachi bamboo flute, nowadays called suizen (吹禅, 'Zen of blowing (the flute)').

Buddhism, also known as Buddha Dharma and Dharmavinaya, is an Indian religion and philosophy based on teachings attributed to the Buddha, a śramaṇa and religious teacher who lived in the 6th or 5th century BCE. It is the world's fourth-largest religion, with about 320 million followers, known as Buddhists, who comprise 4.1% of the global population.

description: Buddhist temple in Kyoto, Japan. Head temple of Honganji-ha school
description_wiki: Nishi Hongan-ji (西本願寺) is a Buddhist temple in Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto, Japan.

description: central figure of Christianity (6 or 4 BC – AD 30 or 33) (5-33)
description_wiki: Jesus (c. 6 to 4 BC – AD 30 or 33), also referred to as Jesus Christ, Jesus of Nazareth, and by various other names and titles, was a 1st-century Jewish preacher and religious leader in the Roman province of Judaea.
Samaria (), the Hellenized form of the Hebrew name Shomron (Hebrew: שֹׁמְרוֹן‎), is used as a historical and biblical name for the central region of the Land of Israel.

description: ritual serving of tea in Japanese culture
description_wiki: The Japanese tea ceremony is a cultural activity known as sadō/chadō (茶道, 'The Way of Tea') or chanoyu (茶の湯), 'Hot water for tea'); the ceremony focuses on the presentation and serving of matcha (抹茶), powdered green tea, and this procedure is called temae (点前).
Ikebana (生け花, 活け花, 'arranging flowers' or 'making flowers alive') is the Japanese art of flower arrangement.
The kimono (着物; Japanese pronunciation: [kʲi.mo.no], lit. 'thing to wear') is a traditional Japanese garment and the national dress of Japan.
Shimane Prefecture (島根県, Shimane-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [ɕiꜜ.ma.ne, ɕi.ma.neꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Chūgoku region of Honshu.

surface_forms: new citizens; lowest class of people

description: conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires from 1904 to 1905
description_wiki: The Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905) was fought between the Russian Empire and the Empire of Japan over rival imperial ambitions in Manchuria and the Korean Empire.

surface_forms: Fifth Division of the Imperial Army
Headquarter of the Fifth Division of the Imperial Army
description_wiki: The 5th Division (第5師団, Dai-go shidan) was an infantry division of the Imperial Japanese Army.

aliases: Army of the Greater Japanese Empire
description: ground-based armed forces of Japan, from 1868 to 1945
description_wiki: The Imperial Japanese Army (IJA) was the principal ground force of the Empire of Japan from 1871 to 1945.

description: capital city of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima (広島市, Hiroshima-shi; English: , also UK: , US: , Japanese: [çiɾoɕima] ) is the capital of Hiroshima Prefecture in Japan.

The term picture bride refers to the practice in the early 20th century of immigrant workers (chiefly Japanese, Okinawan, and Korean) in Hawaii and the West Coast of the United States and Canada, as well as Brazil selecting brides from their native countries via a matchmaker, who paired bride and groom using only photographs and family recommendations of the possible candidates.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.
